title: Exomicrum congestum
taxonomy: Exomicrum congestum is classified within the family of flowering plants, specifically in the order of Ericales. It belongs to the genus Exomicrum and is a species of flowering plant. Exomicrum congestum is native to the Western Ghats region of India and is a part of the Eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This species is classified as a shrub, typically growing up to 2 meters in height. The classification of Exomicrum congestum is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.

intro: Exomicrum congestum, also known as Black Saloop, is a plant species in the family Menispermaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
